meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no understanding of APIs (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills: Master APIs for Your First SaaS Project
content.seo_description: Master API integration by leveraging Claude Code and Antigravity. A high-level, student-focused case study to bridge the gap between theory and production.
content.answer_block: To overcome API ignorance, treat the API as a structural dependency rather than a black box. Use Claude Code to map JSON schemas directly into your codebase, and employ Antigravity to automate the generation of robust, error-handled client wrappers. This modular approach turns complex documentation into functional, testable code.
content.intro:
You are staring at a wall of documentation, feeling the weight of a thousand endpoints you don't understand. The ambition to build your first SaaS is high, but the fear of 'breaking the internet'—or just your own app—is paralyzing. You have the logic, but the invisible bridge between your code and the world’s data remains uncrossed.

This isn't a failure of intelligence; it’s a failure of tooling. By mastering Claude Code and Antigravity, you stop being a student who 'guesses' how to connect services and start acting like a lead engineer who orchestrates them. We are going to dismantle the mystery of APIs, one CLI command at a time.

steps.title: Architecting the API Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define the data structure. Use Claude Code to ingest the API documentation and output a clean TypeScript interface.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API documentation and generate a strict TypeScript interface for the primary GET request. Include JSDoc comments for every field.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./api-docs.md && claude write 'Create a types.ts file with the interface generated from the documentation.'

steps.title: Automating Client Wrapper Generation
steps.description: Avoid manual HTTP calls. Use Antigravity to generate a standard, error-resilient client wrapper that handles authentication and status codes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Axios-based client wrapper for this API. Include automatic handling for 401 Unauthorized errors and 429 rate limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a client.ts wrapper using the previously defined interfaces.'

steps.title: Implementing Middleware for Resilience
steps.description: Add a layer of safety by implementing middleware that logs API interactions for easier debugging during your development cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add a logging middleware to our client wrapper that captures request latency and payload size for every API call.

steps.title: Mocking API Responses for Testing
steps.description: Don't burn through your API quota while testing. Use Claude Code to create a mock server based on your schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock server using MSW (Mock Service Worker) based on our TypeScript interfaces to simulate API responses.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize MSW with handlers for our defined API endpoints.'

steps.title: Production-Ready Error Handling
steps.description: Ensure your app doesn't crash on bad input. Implement robust validation using your defined types.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current client implementation and add Zod validation to ensure all incoming API data matches our expected schema.

pro_tips: Always define your error types as strictly as your success types; most bugs hide in the catch block.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your API client before you ever call the real endpoint.
Never hardcode API keys; force Claude Code to implement a .env validation schema during setup.
When an API is unstable, use Antigravity to implement an exponential backoff strategy automatically.
Treat your API client as a standalone package inside your repo to keep your business logic decoupled.
content.next_steps: After completing this, deploy your client wrapper to a private npm registry or a local monorepo workspace to reuse it across future side projects.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the professional workflow required to integrate any service with confidence. Stop fearing documentation and start building the scalable infrastructure that will define your career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
